rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JAMES E TUCKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 06/06/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) Notice must be given to the Department of Health Care Services if the conservatee received Medi-Cal benefits. 3) The deferred assessment fees ($2,400 in total*) must be paid on a prorated basis. *See assessment orders for 2013, 2017 & 2019 court investigations. It should be noted that the $2,400 check submitted by the petitioner bounced. No payment has been remitted since the Court's notice served on the petitioner on 07/10/2023. 4) If the petitioner intends to request her fees, the petition must be amended with supporting time records. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/23. (No earlier availability.) 1. The will directed all of the residue to be given to the trustee of the Melvin Fleming Revocable Trust. Under what authority can the Court order distribution to the individuals? 2. The pleading does not disclose the value of the estate as of the date of the decedent's death, only the cash on hand at the end of "account" period (although accounting was waived). 3. The I&A filed on 9/22/22 shows that the probate referee commission was $75, but no Attachment 2 was attached to that I&A. What did the referee appraise? 4. Exhibit D, referred to in paragraph 15, is the letter from the DHCS, not the FTB. 5. Paragraph 22 is confusing, as Executor alleges notice WAS GIVEN to the DCVC, pursuant to Probate Code Section 9202(b)...? (That Code section also applies if an heir or beneficiary was PREVIOUSLY so confined, not just currently.) 6. Paragraph 8 refers to the "remaining" compensation to the attorney. Did the executor already pay the attorney a portion of the statutory fee? (Why is the attorney praying for the compensation (paragraph 19 of the report)?) A courtesy copy of the amendment to the second amended petition and proof of service of notice of the amendment to the second amended petition must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: (1) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] However, if the attorney has not accepted any fee yet, and the reduced fee of $5,000 is all that is being requested, Exhibit A will be disregarded, as it is not a "Summary of Account" in any event or a proper accounting of all of the required detailed schedules in support of a statutory fee not based solely on the I&A. (2) In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. [LRSF 14.15 A.] (3)Letters TESTAMENTARY, not of Administration, were issued on 3/28/22. (4) Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILSON PENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: REPOSTED 9/15/2023 at 9:37 am. Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues have not been cleared since this petition was filed. (1) Further allegation whether there has been timely filed Creditor's Claim and if it is or is not verified claim. Petition also needs to set forth reason for delay in handling the claim and the circumstances justifying the expenditures ($48,851). advanced by a relative in absence of timely filed claim. aid by aunt is or is not verified claim and its reasonableness. Paragraph #20 merely seeks approval of the reimbursement to the relative without sufificient information. ; however, the court has no further information such as the reasonableness and verified/non-verified claim. Prayer does not include the request, either. Need clarification. (2) Need allegation that the estate funds are fully covered by FDIC. Appears cash funds in on account exceeds $250K. (3) Proposed Order to be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY EVERETT MOATS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 05/31/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-120 form (including proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. 3) No inventory and appraisal is on file. 4) The financial account statement verifying the ending cash balance must be filed (see LRSF 14.34(A)) with a declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A). 5) The beginning date of the account must be the date of the decedent's death. LRSF 14.34. 6) The accounting format and each schedule must comply with Probate Code sections 1061-1063. 7) Allegations required by section 1064(a)(1)-(5) must be provided. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN MILDRED NELSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The addresses of the heirs listed in the supplement filed on 8/24/23 were not set forth. (2) Addresses for the other heirs, whose whereabouts were initially unknown, were also not set forth in any verified pleading. (3) The instructions regarding remote appearance procedures must be ATTACHED to the Notice of Hearing, which is the document that shows the case and hearing information. Merely serving the procedures alone is not sufficient. (Furthermore, the "Procedures for Making a Remote Access Appearance by Zoom and Courtcall" filed on 8/17/23 does not even have a copy of the procedures attached to it to verify that the correct instructions were served on the parties. And notice of the instructions was reportedly mailed to "Susan" Elna Olson Ingebritson.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 10/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (After remaining procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required, as notice has not been waived for the "unknown issue" of the decedent's paternal uncle Carl E. Johanson and paternal first cousin once removed Nancy P. Olson.)

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/2023. 1) No DE-147 form is on file. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BYRON R. MEYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: REPOSTED 9/26/2023. Matter is continued to 10/10/2023 per the judicial officer after review. Letters of Special Administration is extended to 10/10/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEROLD M. GRODSKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No original will or codicil has been lodged. (2) A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (3) A proposed personal representative is required to sign and verify all of her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [CRC 7.103(c).] (4) Was the predeceased daughter, Jamie Grodsky, not survived by issue? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
